headline: Exxon Is Down Since the Iran Conflict. These Energy Stocks Are Bigger Winners.
summary: For all the drama in oil markets since the recent conflict in Iran began, stocks of U.S. oil majors such as  Exxon  Mobil—and even those of many oil producers—haven’t budged much.  In fact, Exxon stock is down from its Friday closing price.  The markets for the fuels those companies produce have been even more volatile than the overall oil market.

headline: Oil Prices Jump Further As Iran War Rages On; These Oil Stocks Stand Out
summary: Oil prices in futures markets hit new crisis highs as the U.S.-Israel war against Iran continued to shut down tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz for a fourth day.  Supply worry boosted the U.S. crude oil price to $75.89 a barrel, topping Monday's peak just above $73.  Oil and natural gas stocks accounted for five of the top 10 S&P 500 performers early Tuesday, though nitrogen fertilizer play CF Industries joined the leaders for a similar reason: about one-third of global trade in urea and ammonia goes through the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: 10-year Treasury yield tops 4.09% as surging oil prices from Iran conflict raise inflation angst
summary: U.S. Treasury yields on Tuesday rose as investors continued to assess the ongoing war in the Middle East.
